item.title: No feasts, no joy: Gazans mark a dark Eid
item.description:
New clothes for children, sacrificial sheep and Eid biscuits, the hallmarks of the Muslim holiday, are all either unaffordable or unavailable in Gaza, casting a shadow over what is usually a time of celebration and joy.

"I go to the market only to look around because I cannot afford to buy anything. Whenever I ask about prices, I return heartbroken," Nadia Abu Shamala, a Palestinian resident of Gaza, told AFP.

item.title: UN Security Council condemns attack on UAE nuclear plant
item.description:
WASHINGTON, May 26 (Reuters) - The United Nations Security Council on Tuesday condemned the attack on Barakah Nuclear Plant in the United Arab Emirates, according to a statement, adding that theattack constituted a violation of international law.

The council did not attribute blame for the attack.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 31 in south Lebanon as Israel expands ground operations
item.description:
Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed 31 people on Tuesday, the Lebanese health ministry said, as Israel said it was intensifying attacks despite a truce in its war with Hezbollah.

The Iran-backed group meanwhile said it faced Israeli troops entering the southern town of Zawtar al-Sharqiyah, as the Israeli military said it was expanding its ground operations.

In a statement, the health ministry said 31 people, including at least four children and three women, were killed in attacks and 40 wounded.

item.title: Tanker reports external explosion off Oman coast, crew safe, UKMTO says
item.description:
DUBAI/OSLO, May 26 (Reuters) - United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations said on Tuesday that a tanker had reported an external explosion on the vessel's port side, close to the waterline, 60 nautical miles off Oman's capital Muscat.

UKMTO said the vessel, identified as the Olympic Life, and its crew were safe, although the tanker reported some bunker fuel had been discharged into the sea. It said the cause of the incident was unknown.

item.title: Israel strikes near Lebanon’s Qaraoun dam as war intensifies: What to know
item.description: The strikes near the large water reservoir follow pressure on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to up attacks on Hezbollah in response to rocket fire and a rising death toll in Lebanon.

item.title: US gave Israel 'tacit agreement' to target senior Hezbollah figures
item.description: While there is no explicit agreement, sources say Washington has offered quiet approval despite a ceasefire reached April 16.

item.title: US-Iran deal could take ‘a few days’ as roadblocks emerge, nuclear talks deferred
item.description: Mediators are scrambling to secure a deal that would end the war, while leaving unresolved the thorniest issues that predated it.

item.title: US-Iran deal could take ‘a few days’ as roadblocks emerge, nuclear talks deferred
item.description: Mediators are scrambling to secure a deal that would end the war, while leaving unresolved the thorniest issues that predated it.

item.title: Cash as leverage: How Iran's push for frozen billions is reshaping diplomacy
item.description: The frozen assets debate is a test of whether the postwar diplomatic track can move toward a durable framework — or whether financial mistrust will evolve into yet another breaking point.

item.title: Cash as leverage: How Iran's push for frozen billions is reshaping diplomacy
item.description: The frozen assets debate is a test of whether the postwar diplomatic track can move toward a durable framework — or whether financial mistrust will evolve into yet another breaking point.
